questions.scenario:
A 28-year-old woman, primigravidarum, GA 10 weeks, little bleeding per vagina with blight red blood. PV: os closed, old blood clot. Ultrasound presents gestational sac, 10 week-size, normal fetal heart activity. What is the appropriate management
Bed rest
Cervical cerclage
Estradiol
Sedatic and narcotic drug
Inhibit with tocolytic
A 28-year-old GA 10 weeks presents with veginal bleeding. PV: os close, uterus 10 weeks size. Present fetal cardiac activity. What is the management
Bed rest
Estradiol supplement
Cervical cerclage
A patient GA third trimester has vaginal bleeding. She has abdominal pain and contraction ถี่ๆ. Ultrasound reveals uterus at fundus, no retroplacental blood clot. What is the most likely diagnosis
Vasa previa
Placenta previa
Uterine rupture
Placenta accreta
Abruption placenta
A woman G2P1001 GA 38 weeks, previous low transverse cesarean section, presents with bleeding per vagina and abdominal pain. PR 120/min, BP 80/60 mmHg. Can palpable fetal part. What is the diagnosis
Placenta previa
Uterine rupture
Abruption placenta
A 40-year-old woman first ANC at GA 12 weeks. She has severe nausea, vomiting, dark urine. PE: fundal 2/3 above pubic symphysis. What is the most proper investigation
Serum β-hCG
Ultrasound
Amniocentesis
Progesterone supplement
A 35-year-old female patient primigravida GA 10 week by LMP presented with blood spotting.  PE: Uterus 20 week size. Ultrasonography revealed numerous cystic appearance. What is the most appropriate management?
Waiting for spontaneous abortion
Misoprostal vaginal suppository
Suction curettage
Fractional curettage
Hysterectomy
A 26-year-old woman primigravida GA 15 week comes for ANC.She concerns that her child might have Down syndrome since her cousin’s child has Down syndrome. What is your management
Reassure that there is no risk
Triple quad screening
Nuchal translucency
Amniocentesis
Chorionic villus sampling
A 36-year-old primigravida GA 30 weeks come for ANC. PE: fundal height 1/4 above umbilicus, FHS 140/min. Ultrasound: single viable fetus, estimated fetal weight less than 10th percentile of normal average GA. Which is the best investigation for fetal surveillance
Fetal movement count
Non stress test
Biophysical profile
Contraction test
Umbilical artery doppler

Oxytocin
Observation
Vacuum extraction
Forceps extraction
Emergency cesarean section
(variable deceleration)What is the management
Drip oxytocin
Amniotomy
Observe
Emergency c/s
Intrauterine resuscitation

Wrong date
Polyhydramnios
Fetal macrosomia
Multiple pregnancy

Beta-blocker
Magnesium sulfate
Calcium channel blocker
Oxytocin receptor antagonist

Urine analysis
Nitrazine paper at perineum
Sonography to evaluate amniotic fluid
Finger examination to estimate cervical dilatation
Speculum for pooling of fluid
What is the absolute contraindication for amniotomy to induce labor
Macrosomia
Polyhydramnios
Prematurity
Transverse lie

A 25-year-old female primigravida GA 35 week, normal previous ANC, presents with persistent headache 3 days. PE: BP 170/110 mmHg, pitting edema, DTR 3+, FHR 140, uterine contraction interval 2 min, duration 50 sec, moderate intensity, Cx dilate...cm, effacement 50%, station 0, intact membrane, urine albumin 2+. After MgSO4 IV, BP 140/90 mmHg. What is the next proper management
Amniotomy
IV dexamethasone
IV antihypertension
Emergency C/S
A 20-year-old woman primigravida GA 38 weeks presents with abdominal pain. PE: BP 150/90 mmHg, puffy eyelid, pretibial edema, no uterine contraction. PV: dilate 3 cm, effacement 20%, station 0, membrane intact. Lab: urine protein 2+. What is the management
Reassure and follow up twice a week
Induction at GA 39 week
Admit and close observe
Admit and induction
Emergency cesarean section
A 20-year-old primigravida at 35 weeks of gestation had a blood pressure of 150/100 mmHg with edema of lower extremities and proteinuria 1+. She developed a generalized tonic-clonic convulsion for 2 minutes. Deep tendon reflex was 3+. Cervix os was closed. Which is the appropriate management
Cesarean section immediately
Order CT scan emergency and consult neurosurgery
Start MgSO4 infusion then try induction with oxytocin
Start MgSO4 infusion then cesarean section in 2-4 hour
Start MgSO4 infusion then try induction with misoprostol
A patient GA… PV: cephalic presentation, prolapsed cord, fully dilated cervix, station 0. Cannot do cesarean section within 30 minutes. What is the proper management
Knee chest position
Forceps extraction
Vacuum extraction
Manual replacement the prolapsed cord above the presenting part

Wait for spontaneous delivery

A woman G1P0 GA… in labor. Second stage for 1 hour. Contraction interval 2 minute, duration 45 sec, station 3+. What is the proper management
Observe progression of labor
Vacuum extraction
Forceps extraction
Emergency cesarean section
A 35-year-old woman, P4004 had vaginal delivered a 4,000 g baby, and had complete placental delivery 1 hr. Postpartum she developed massive vaginal hemorrhage. BT 37 C, RR 20, PR 100, BP 80/50 mmHg. Uterine fundus is 1/4 above umbilicus, poor uterine contraction. No vaginal or cervical laceration. After fluid resuscitation and urine catheterization, what is the most appropriate management

Dopamine infusion
Oxytocin and uterine massage

GA 37 weeks transverse position. What is next management

External podalic
Wait
